title: pyrus ciliata
intro: Pyrus ciliata, also known as the Himalayan wild apple or Tibetan wild apple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Himalayan region. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids that exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Pyrus ciliata include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit anti-cancer properties, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
